equipment: Fixed dumbbell pairs: 1 kg, 2.5 kg, 5 kg
2 adjustable rods (1.5 kg each, weighed) + disks: 4x 0.5 kg, 4x 1.25 kg, 4x 2.5 kg (2 of each size per hand)
athlete.notes: Home training. Weights are per hand. DISKS ARE NOT CHANGED MID-SESSION: each session's equipment list states the one rod setting to make before starting, and every two-handed exercise uses a fixed pair (1 / 2.5 / 5 kg). Rod maths — take the disks on ONE side, double it (two sides per hand), add the 1.5 kg rod: nothing → 1.5, 0.5 → 2.5, 1.25 → 4, 0.5+1.25 → 5, 2.5 → 6.5, 2.5+0.5 → 7.5, 2.5+1.25 → 9, 2.5+1.25+0.5 → 10. Knock knees (mild, noted Jul 2026) → cue knees OUT on every squat/lunge/step-up, and keep glute-med work (side leg raises, single-leg bridges) in every week. Left knee: mild discomfort on twists (noted Jul 2026) — knee work stays in pain-free range, no knee caving inward, no loaded twisting. Desk job (sits all day) → hip-flexor stretch every cooldown. Goal: start jogging — building calf capacity now; add a Saturday walk-jog day after 2-4 knee-quiet weeks. Sessions use supersets (blocks sharing a group interleave sets). SIX DAYS, THREE HARD: Mon/Wed/Fri are the strength sessions; Tue/Thu/Sat are short easy days (mobility, core, calves, walking) that build the habit and the jogging base without adding recovery load; Sunday is a real rest day. Keep easy days easy — if they start feeling like workouts, cut them back. Long-term plan: see ROADMAP.md.
